intro: Koeleria subaristata, also known as slender hair grass, is a species of grass native to the mountainous regions of China, characterized by its slender stems and narrow leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria subaristata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Koeleria subaristata include decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
